programme.title: History Detectives: Marion Carpenter Camera, Drug Smuggling Doll, Florida Map
programme.desc: Investigating a camera that could have belonged to the first woman photographer at the White House. Families of the Confederate South may have used this doll to smuggle medicine past the Northern Blockade.

programme.title: Rivers of Life: The Amazon
programme.desc: The Amazon, the greatest river system on Earth, amasses one-fifth of Earth's freshwater as it flows east from the Andes to the Atlantic.

programme.title: Breakthrough: The Ideas That Changed the World: The Smartphone
programme.desc: Dial in to the fascinating history of the smartphone, from its roots in Morse Code to 2007, when Apple unveiled the first-ever iPhone. Plus, see how the next generation of smartphones will allow us to communicate through them just by thinking.

programme.title: Nova: Killer Floods
programme.desc: Uncover geologic fingerprints of colossal floods that violently reshaped the ancient world. Follow geologists around the globe as they reconstruct catastrophic Ice Age floods more powerful than all the world's top ten rivers combined.

programme.title: Wild Weather

programme.title: Masterpiece Classic: Howards End
programme.desc: Margaret is undaunted by Henry’s past misdeeds. Helen leaves abruptly for Europe. Margaret and Tibby get Henry to help resolve issues at Howards End – where the three families have a showdown.

programme.title: The Story of China: Golden Age

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Promise

programme.desc: When Fiona gets dumped she heads for a week in Vermont at her Dad's country Inn. There she runs into her ex with his new girlfriend. She strikes a deal with the Inn's chef, Derek, to be her 'boyfriend' for the week.

programme.title: Movie: Love at First Dance
programme.desc: A woman is tasked with teaching Manhattan's former 'Most Eligible Bachelor' how to dance for his extravagant, society wedding. But as the dance lessons progress, complications ensue when feelings begin to develop between them.

programme.title: The Office: Stress Relief
programme.desc: After Dwight's safety seminar goes awry, he must make amends to the stressed-out office. Michael tries a number of ways to get his employees to relax before learning that he is the number one stressor at work.

programme.title: The Office: Casual Friday
programme.desc: Michael has to mediate a dispute within his new sales team. Meanwhile, trouble brews in the office when several employees take the term 'casual' Fridays too loosely.

programme.title: The Office: Gossip
programme.desc: Michael feels left out as the rest of the office gossips about the summer interns. While Michael searches for the next juicy rumour to share, Andy struggles with heterosexuality.

programme.title: The Office: Koi Pond
programme.desc: The office plans a haunted house for the children of the community. Michael falls into a koi pond while at an important meeting. Pam and Andy try cold calling for more sales.

programme.title: The Office: Murder
programme.desc: Michael is troubled by rumours about Dunder Mifflin, and forces the office into a day of strange diversions. Andy figures out that the chief obstacle he has to overcome is his own awkwardness.

programme.title: The Office: The Chump
programme.desc: Michael is surprisingly cheerful after learning some bad news about Donna. New parents, Pam and Jim, have trouble staying awake at work.

programme.title: The Office: The Seminar
programme.desc: When Andy's sales are at his lowest ever, he gets desperate to hold a small business seminar.

programme.title: The Office: Threat Level Midnight
programme.desc: Michael is finally ready to present his long-awaited action movie 'Threat Level Midnight,' to his co-workers and his love interest.

programme.title: The Office: Michael's Last Dundies
programme.desc: Michael trains Deangeloe on how to take over the job of hosting the Dundie Awards.

programme.title: The Office: Search Committee
programme.desc: When the search begins for a new manager, Jim heads the search committee that interviews the candidates.

programme.title: The Office: Search Committee
programme.desc: When the search begins for a new manager, Jim heads the search committee that interviews the candidates.

programme.title: The Office: The Incentive
programme.desc: After a motivational speech, Robert challenges the office to double their sales. After the new manager promises the unthinkable, everyone in the office suddenly gets motivated to sell. Pam finds an unlikely pregnancy friend.

programme.title: The Office: Diversity Day
programme.desc: A consultant arrives to teach the staff about tolerance and diversity, and Michael decides to impart his own knowledge on the topic. Jim struggles after losing his biggest yearly commission to Dwight.

programme.title: The Office: The Convict
programme.desc: Michael tries to be supportive after he learns that a new employee is an ex-convict.

programme.title: South Park: You're Not Yelping

programme.title: South Park: Member Berries

programme.title: South Park: Wieners Out

programme.title: South Park: Bass to Mouth
programme.desc: The kids' most scandalous secrets are being leaked on an outrageous new gossip website.

programme.title: South Park: The Ring
programme.desc: Kenny takes his new girlfriend to a Jonas Brothers' concert where they each get purity rings.

programme.title: South Park: The China Problem
programme.desc: With the rest of the American people haunted by the memory of a recent tragic event, only Butters will stand with Cartman as he confronts the Chinese.

programme.title: South Park: Erection Day
programme.desc: The South Park Elementary School Talent Show is coming up and Jimmy can't wait to perform his comedy routine. But first he must find a way to gain control of his raging hormones.

programme.title: South Park: Broadway Bro Down
programme.desc: After Randy takes Sharon to see a hit musical, he becomes Broadway's biggest fan.

programme.title: South Park: Fat Butt and Pancake Head

programme.desc: A police officer, who has been married three times, lands a girl half his age. While his relationship with his new girl blossoms, he is undergoing a bitter divorce from his third wife, who unexpectedly ends up dead.

programme.title: The Closer: Problem Child
programme.desc: The case of a troubled missing boy quickly turns into a homicide investigation.

programme.title: The Closer: Sudden Death
programme.desc: When Sanchez's little brother is shot, the team turns its efforts to finding out if this is related to a past case of the detective's. Meanwhile, a reporter accuses Brenda and her squad of only working on cases that are connected to the rich and powerful.

programme.title: The Closer: Split Ends
programme.desc: Deputy Chief Johnson has her hands full trying to solve a murder when a hairdresser on a movie set is found dead. Meanwhile, Brenda's folks are in town pressuring her to set a wedding date.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: The Lion Sleeps Tonight
programme.desc: A lion breaks loose in Seattle, leaving casualties in its wake. Lexie hears Mark talking about moving in with Julie and Teddy deals with coming to terms with Henry's death.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Support System
programme.desc: Christina presses Owen to confess the details of his affair. Meanwhile, Mark takes his new authority very seriously when he steps in as chief for the day and butts heads with Richard over a patient's live transplant.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: The Girl With No Name
programme.desc: The doctors work on a Jane Doe, who turns out to be the subject of a case that gained national interest; Cristina proves to be the highest in demand as the residents begin their interviews for post-residency positions at prospective hospitals.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Moment of Truth
programme.desc: After eight years of practice, the residents head to San Francisco for their oral boards; sparks fly between Catherine and Richard when they bump into each other at the hotel; Bailey intervenes after Owen and Teddy get into a heated argument.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Let the Bad Times Roll
programme.desc: The residents agonize over their oral boards, reliving every answer they gave during their exams. The doctors work on a patient who is missing one third of his skull.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Migration
programme.desc: The residents determine their future as they make their final decisions regarding post residency positions. Mark admits to Derek that he is torn between his love for Lexie and the fact that Julia can give him everything he's ever wanted.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: The Bombing of Oklahoma City: McVeigh's Shocking Confession (Part 2)
programme.desc: In an exclusive prison interview, Timothy McVeigh himself describes how events in his personal life and the world around him inspired him to kill his fellow Americans. We also hear from survivors of the bombing and family members of the victims.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Capturing the Unabomber
programme.desc: In a new interview, Ted Kacyznski's brother, the man who ultimately turned him in to the authorities, takes us behind the hunt that led to the capture of 'The Unabomber.'

programme.title: How It Really Happened: The Mystery of the Gainesville Murders (Part 1)
programme.desc: During a terrifying three-day period in late August 1990, five college students were found brutally murdered in Gainesville, Florida.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: The Mystery of the Gainesville Murders (Part 2)
programme.desc: After five murders it would take investigators months - and the discovery of a campsite, a screwdriver and an audio tape - before they identified who the serial killer was.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: The Strange Case of Jeffrey Dahmer
programme.desc: The chilling account of a man who survived his encounter with the notorious serial killer, who managed to evade police scrutiny for years.

programme.title: Forensic Files: House Hunting
programme.desc: When a successful real estate agent is brutally murdered, panicked townspeople begin looking over their shoulders and locking their doors.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shoot to Thrill
programme.desc: A teenager returns home after a night out with friends, and discovers his father's body lying in a pool of blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Kidnapping
programme.desc: A family vacation turns into a manhunt for a missing wife and baby. When their worst fears are confirmed, police must switch gears to find a killer. With computer tracing, finger and palm print analysis, and DNA testing, investigators can solve the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sands of Crime
programme.desc: A college co-ed is abducted from the parking lot of her apartment building, and her body was found later that day. She’d been sexually assaulted and then shot at point blank range.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sunday's Wake
programme.desc: When a little girl got sick and died, investigators were stumped. Scientists would travel half-way around the world before finding the answer in two unlikely places.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shattered Dreams
programme.desc: A car is discovered in a drainage ditch with two bullet-riddled bodies inside, and a fingerprint from a latex glove provides key evidence investigators need to determine where the crime took place as well as the identity of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dockter Visit
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran is impeded when detectives' prime suspect provides a solid alibi, but forensic scientists are able to make a key discovery due to the killer's preference for a distinctive style of footwear.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A woman suffering from depression apparently took her own life. But her sister told police, that a year before her death, she said if anything were to happen to her, there was a note in the china cabinet. Investigators found the note and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Finger Pane
programme.desc: Texas City police fear the work of a serial killer after two young women are found dead in the same field. They have little to go on until a third woman comes forward claiming she's the one who got away.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Good as Gold
programme.desc: Two young women are killed by a mysterious intruder. Investigators hope that his unusual brand of cigarettes and new advances in DNA technology can paint a picture of a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Freedom Fighter
programme.desc: 15 years after a murder and conviction, a letter from prison sets off a chain of events that has investigators exhuming a body and looking for evidence to find out if they convicted the real killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dog Day Afternoon
programme.desc: A woman is found dead in her Florida home, and evidence at the scene is scarce. All signs point to burglary. The case goes cold until police get a tip from a canine behaviorist who points them in a different direction.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Moss Not Grass
programme.desc: A young woman is found dead on a golf course in the Bahamas, and the evidence leads police to two suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Material Witness
programme.desc: A teenager goes missing after an evening of horseback riding, and her body is found a month later, three miles from her home.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Garden of Evil
programme.desc: A popular disc jockey is found murdered in a community garden. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert lead police to the killer... and he’d been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sunday School Ambush
programme.desc: Forensic science helps investigators unravel a twisted tale of lust, greed and deception.

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade
programme.desc: A daily dose of news, weather, sports, entertainment, finance and travel, in a fast paced, approachable style.

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade

programme.title: The Killer Truth: Homicide at Home
programme.desc: A love triangle gone awry leaves Mississippi investigators searching for who was holding the gun that killed Avis Banks and her unborn child.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: John Lennon Assassination
programme.desc: In 1980, the entire world was stunned by the murder of John Lennon. Was Mark Chapman a cold-blooded killer or a seriously deranged young man? We will backtrack through the lives of both men to set the scene through interviews and footage.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: Reagan Assassination Attempt
programme.desc: In 1981, a gunman fires six shots at Ronald Reagan. What follows is a chaotic rush to save Reagan's life, confusion at the White House, and an urgent investigation to find out if this is a wider plot, or the work of a lone assassin.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Novel Idea
programme.desc: When a frantic call from a man claiming his wife fell down the stairs brings investigators to their home, they are concerned by the unusually large number of bloodstains, and how big they are.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Chief Suspect
programme.desc: The wife of a police officer is murdered in her own home. After a decade a newly formed cold case unit took a new look at the case, and using a fragment of a 911 call, they caught the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Water Hazard
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue, a flaw on a mailing envelope, exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people to get the one person he wanted.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shell Game
programme.desc: A tiny clue, no bigger than a thumbtack, held all the information investigators needed to put a trio of cold-blooded killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sole Survivor
programme.desc: A college student vanished without a trace, and dirt on a pair of shoes reveals the extent of one man's deception.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Insignificant Others
programme.desc: A man's wife had taken her own life, and his college sweetheart had killed herself in much the same fashion fourteen years earlier. Investigators had to determine if this was a coincidence or an attempt to get away with murder twice.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis: Texas Toast
programme.desc: Tricia and JJ both have chances to redeem themselves while Memphis continues to struggle against Texas. The competition is more intense than ever and one wrong move could mean it's game over for the Memphis crew.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis: Beat Me in St. Louis
programme.desc: The MSO heads to St. Louis to race the slick streets against a team of even slicker racers led by Dynamite Dave. Memphis came out on top the last time these teams faced off, now the St. Louis crew is ready to even the score on their home turf.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis: Full Throttle: Sooner Than Later
programme.desc: With added facts and bonus content: MSO just wrapped up their battle with St. Louis but get a callout from Oklahoma before they can leave town. Memphis isn't ready to back down but they must rally if they want to make it worth their while.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis: Second City Hustlers
programme.desc: As their battle with Chicago approaches, the MSO scrambles to fix Heifer after losing her to a fender bender. JJ finds a '65 Malibu they can use, but Chicago is stacked with fast cars and street sense, so running an unknown may be out of the question.

programme.title: Fast N' Loud: Flippin' the Bird
programme.desc: The guys face a seven-day deadline to raise their '67 Firebird from the ashes. Meanwhile, Richard heads to the boondocks of Tokio, TX, to search for a rare '51 VW Samba Bus. Finally, NFL star DeMarcus Ware shops for an authentic Gas Monkey hotrod.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers: Diesel Nation Celebrates Memorial Day
programme.desc: In the season premiere, the Diesel Brothers celebrate Memorial Day and salute the men and women of the Armed Forces the best way they know how: epic builds, special celebrity guests and a crazy stunt to cap off the night.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers: There Will Be Mud
programme.desc: Heavy D, Diesel Dave and the Dieselsellerz crew work to finish their epic monster mud truck for the giveaway, and The Muscle scrambles to find a buyer for the flashy Denali he bought without Heavy D's permission.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis: Second City Hustlers
programme.desc: As their battle with Chicago approaches, the MSO scrambles to fix Heifer after losing her to a fender bender. JJ finds a '65 Malibu they can use, but Chicago is stacked with fast cars and street sense, so running an unknown may be out of the question.

programme.title: Fast N' Loud: Flippin' the Bird
programme.desc: The guys face a seven-day deadline to raise their '67 Firebird from the ashes. Meanwhile, Richard heads to the boondocks of Tokio, TX, to search for a rare '51 VW Samba Bus. Finally, NFL star DeMarcus Ware shops for an authentic Gas Monkey hotrod.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers: Diesel Nation Celebrates Memorial Day
programme.desc: In the season premiere, the Diesel Brothers celebrate Memorial Day and salute the men and women of the Armed Forces the best way they know how: epic builds, special celebrity guests and a crazy stunt to cap off the night.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers: Motors and Rotors
programme.desc: Heavy D, Diesel Dave and the Dieselsellerz crew scramble to finish a six-door stretched F650 Expedition Camper for a big client. And Heavy D shocks the team with his latest flip- a diesel-powered experimental helicopter.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers: Monster Jump Live
programme.desc: A salute to the military with this action-packed night of insane stunts. Heavy D hopes to make history by jumping the Brodozer over a flying airplane, while Diesel Dave, the Muscle, and others attempts stunts of their own.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Snake Researcher
programme.desc: Mike wrangles snakes and steps into the shoes of hard working bait and lobster fishermen in Maine.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Turkey Inseminator
programme.desc: Mike crawls through rat drops, falling insulation and debris to make a house more energy efficient and he visits a turkey farm where he gets to suck semen out of a bunch of large-breasted birds.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Sheep Castrator
programme.desc: Mike works at an automobile dealership where the job is sawing down a giant concrete wall to make room for a new washbay, then heads to a sheep farm where he tries his hand at sheep shearing & castrating before getting treated to Rocky Mountain Oysters.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Maggot Farmer
programme.desc: Mike travels to Congress, Arizona, to try his hand at moving a giant cactus before a storm comes in. Then Mike heads to Idaho where he visits a special kind of farm, one that raises maggots.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Spray Insulation Technician
programme.desc: Mike Rowe gets dirty with a team of airport runway painters, examines dirty diapers for bad smells and then teams up with a group of spray insulation technicians that get dirty to keep our houses warm.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Leech Trapper
programme.desc: Mike teams up with the boys from Bartos Bait & Fish to catch leeches that will later be sold for fish bait; then it's off to the Green Tripe factory in Hollister, CA where Mike helps make BARF (Biologically Appropriate Raw Food) for dogs.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Abandoned Mine Plugger
programme.desc: Mike travels to Ohio to check on the Common Tern population and then he finds himself in Northern California plugging an abandoned mineshaft.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Special Effects Artist
programme.desc: Mike exposes the fascinating secrets behind the dirty world of special effects. He explores this very strange world as special effects artist, Toby Sells, transforms him into a real life zombie. Also, Mike gets dirty harvesting kelp.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Water Softener Technician
programme.desc: Mike travels to Moorhead, Minnesota, to work as a water softener technician. Then he heads to the Westin St. Francis hotel in San Francisco where he takes on the job of a coin washer.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Floating Fish Factory
programme.desc: Mike travels to Alaska and explores how fish are prepared aboard a processing ship.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Steam Ship Cleaner
programme.desc: Travelling to San Diego, Mike joins forces with two dirty guys who have mastered the art of hydro seeding. Before diving into the mailbag to answer viewer's questions, Mike rolls up his sleeves and gets dirty on a steam ship that's over 100 years old.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: The Pleasure Principle
programme.desc: Geoffrey settles back home. Lisa confronts Usman. Ed leaves the Philippines. Avery and Ash spend time with his son. Darcey rethinks her priorities. Stephanie and Erika say goodbye. Yolanda wants answers. David continues his quest for Lana.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Hard Habit To Break
programme.desc: David tries to get closer to Lana. Avery says goodbye to Ash. Geoffrey finds himself caught in the middle of a sticky situation. Ed returns home. Stephanie reunites with her mom, and drama ensues when Lisa meets with Usman's elders.

programme.desc: Laura tries to get on the last flight out of Ecuador, Dean gives an update on his relationship with Tarik. Omar talks with Avery's dad who's in prison. Anna gives her family at-home haircuts. While Daya works at the hospital, Brett is stay-at-home dad.

programme.desc: Fernanda has a virtual date. Tiffany and Ronald seek counseling with a therapist. Jesse helps a friend who's in the hospital with COVID-19. Rachel and Jon talk with a lawyer about new delays in their visa. Jenny and Larry sing karaoke for their neighbors.

programme.title: Trading Spaces: A Surprise in the Truck
programme.desc: A few special guests bring many design surprises to this heartfelt and humorous episode. A couple of DIY design enthusiasts trade spaces with their neighbors and friends, a family whose toddler has overcome cancer.

programme.title: Trading Spaces: Pizza Boards and Industrial Doors
programme.desc: Legacy designer Laurie Smith joins Sabrina Soto in the suburbs of Baltimore, Maryland as two sets of young parents prepare to redo a room in each other's homes. One designer's ambitious plans may cause some super-sized concerns.

programme.title: Little People, Big World: Little People, Big Goals
programme.desc: Matt and Amy are on shaky ground after meeting with their divorce lawyers, but Matt thinks that more space might make it easier to co-exist peacefully on the farm. Zach finds a way to use his love of soccer to bring awareness to dwarf athleletics.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Swim Shady
programme.desc: The guys hit some bad reception working at a desk. Plus, tonight's big loser takes photos of a water aerobics class, but ends up drowning in shame.

programme.desc: The guys compete in their first four-way challenge. Later, see what happens when they try to lead a kid's art class, and which loser fails so badly that he's hit with an on-the-spot punishment that leaves him seeing red.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Medium, Well Done

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Chick Magnet

programme.title: Las Vegas: Father of the Bride

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: The Lord Is Back
programme.desc: Scott, Benny and Miki are almost finished with their latest flip property in Malibu, so Scott recruits a potential new team member, Willa Ford, to help with the finishing interior design touches.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Family Matters
programme.desc: With Jed Smith officially purchased and the new layout for the property agreed upon, Scott is eager to get started with demo right away, but Willa and Miki are at odds.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Miki vs. Malibu
programme.desc: After visiting a mega-flip property being built by Scott's real estate mentor, Ilan, Scott is inspired to redo the pool at his personal residence.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Jungle Rules
programme.desc: With Miki feeling disrespected and a potential HOA shutdown looming, the team now faces major personal and logistical problems. Scott helps French Montana transform one of his rooms into a jungle-themed theater.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Unexpected Help
programme.desc: With the HOA shutdown still keeping Jed Smith at a standstill, Willa and Benny renovate their offices. Scott looks for ways to make up for lost time and brings in outside help without telling Benny or Miki.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: More Money, More Problems
programme.desc: Scott and team encounter a problem at Jed Smith that could jeopardize the entire project. Scott attempts to get away from all the drama at Jed Smith by working with Willa to redesign his daughter Penelope's bedroom.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Thank You
programme.desc: Scott contemplates his next move as the Jed Smith project has become more than he bargained for. Meanwhile, Scott and Ilan continue to disagree about what to do with the grotto at the Mark Wahlberg flip project.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: The Race to Finish
programme.desc: The team races to complete Jed Smith in order to get it listed and sold before the end of summer. Scott and his long-term girlfriend Sofia Richie look to buy a house together.

programme.title: The Daily Pop
programme.desc: We check out the newest trends you need to try and discuss the hottest new dating terms for summer.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Return From Paradise
programme.desc: With only a few days left in paradise, Kendall and Kylie try to sort out their differences while Kim and Khloé help Kourtney try to forget Scott and get her mojo back. When the family returns home, a visit from Scott throws them all for a loop.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Big Launch
programme.desc: When Kourtney isn't ready for a big product launch in NYC, her sisters help her get her head in the game. Meanwhile, a very pregnant Kim accidently upsets her best friend Jonathan. Kim makes sure Kylie is making the most of her newfound opportunities.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Fear the Unknown
programme.desc: Knowing her family's history with cancer, Kris wants the girls to take a genetic test to know their risks, but not everyone is on board. Meanwhile Kendall struggles to find a balance in her relationship with Caitlyn.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Miscommunication
programme.desc: Kris decides the family needs 'Communication Therapy,' but her plans backfire when she misses one of the sessions. Kylie is finally ready to celebrate her new home but her family's bossy ways get in the way of her plans.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Guy's Carnival Games
programme.desc: Guy Fieri invites four carnival food-loving chefs to play carnival classics, Grocery Games-style. First, he turns a popular strength test game into a finesse challenge to determine which ingredient the chefs must feature in their classic carnival treat.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Grill Masters
programme.desc: Four grill masters hit Flavortown for some red-hot grillin' games. First, they must think small and fit all the ingredients for their hometown grilled special into ultra-tiny grocery bags. Next, they play a game of Grocery Golf.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: GGG's Salute To Summer
programme.desc: To celebrate America and the people who have served it, Guy Fieri invites four chef veterans from different branches of the military to compete in two summer-themed games.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Salute the Troops
programme.desc: Guy honours America's men and women in uniform by inviting four military vets with a culinary second-calling to play his GGG games. They'll be joining forces with the Messlords, a group of DDD chefs who travel the world cooking for our troops.

programme.title: Bakeaway Camp with Martha Stewart: Suddenly Stormy
programme.desc: The four remaining campers are up bright and early for a breakfast-themed Counselor's Challenge introduced by host Jesse Palmer. Using foraged ingredients like lavender, rosemary, lemon, honey, and pine nuts, the campers must make an elevated breakfast.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Building Bridges
programme.desc: Nothing in Alaska comes easy, especially when it comes to livestock. The Kilchers look to build a bridge to help their herds graze more easily before the summer floods create a natural barrier.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Earthquake at the Edge
programme.desc: Natural disaster strikes the Kilchers while Atz is already reeling from his near-death experience after falling from a cliff.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Winds of Change
programme.desc: Harsh Alaskan weather is battering the Kilchers after the earthquake and puts previous commodities at stake that are vital for survival.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Dead in the Water

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier: Open Season: Keeping Kilcher Tradition
programme.desc: In an effort to keep Kilcher tradition alive, August goes on his first-ever deer hunt, while Etienne experiences his first big game hunt. Otto and Charlotte head to Switzerland to discover the family history.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Digging Out
programme.desc: After one of the worst snowstorms in several years, the Kilchers will need to dig out from underneath piles upon piles of heavy snow before they can get started on an ambitious project that will see the homestead completely re-done.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Season of the Sun
programme.desc: Spring is an idyllic time in Alaska especially compared to the harshness of winter. However, the homesteaders need to use this time to stock up on provisions or risk winter peril.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier: Open Season: Before Winter Strikes
programme.desc: When Otto's and Eivin's hunt for ptarmigan doesn't go as planned, Atz Lee and Jane hope to have better luck tracking caribou. Then, as winter lurks, the Kilchers prepare the homestead, and Atz Lee and Atz Sr. build a weather station on Perl Island.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Stir Crazy
programme.desc: The Kilchers are on the hunt for more meat after being caught in a particularly nasty winter. While the hunt is necessary, it is fraught with peril as they navigate the frozen wilderness.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier - The Frozen Edge: Building Bridges
programme.desc: Nothing in Alaska comes easy, especially when it comes to livestock. The Kilchers look to build a bridge to help their herds graze more easily before the summer floods create a natural barrier.

programme.desc: Natural disaster strikes the Kilchers while Atz is already reeling from his near-death experience after falling from a cliff.

programme.title: Tanked!: A Guiding Light to Fitness

programme.title: The Zoo: Andre the Baby Goat

programme.desc: Intelligence is on the track of a pill mill spreading in the suburbs of Chicago and the doctor enabling it after the death of Judge Tommy Wells' daughter leads them to it. Voight discovers who had been leaking the 21st's information.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Rabbit Hole
programme.desc: Halstead and Camila's partying puts him in the middle of a drug-related homicide, forcing him to choose between his loyalty to his unit and his girl. Voight discovers the mole in his unit.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Confidential
programme.desc: While trying to find the pimp, Intelligence discovers the women's shelter where he scouts girls. Burgess moves the case forward with a series of confidential tips but risks putting her informant in harm's way.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Captive
programme.desc: When Atwater disappears at the house of a convicted felon, the evidence doesn't look promising as his belongings and blood have been left behind. Intelligence rallies to rescue him before it's too late. Atwater struggles for survival.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Chasing Monsters
programme.desc: A gang from El Salvador infiltrates Chicago and Intelligence works with a visiting detective to put an end to the crew's brutal tactics. Antonio finds himself getting close to the Salvadorian detective Marcella Gomez but learns she has a hidden motive.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Snitch
programme.desc: While investigating a drug related homicide, Intelligence is stonewalled by the 'no snitch' culture of the neighborhood.

programme.title: The Simpsons: 'Tis the 30th Season
programme.desc: After failed Black Friday shopping, Marge is determined to fix Christmas but Homer and the kids decide to surprise her by going on vacation to a Floridian resort that turns out to be a knock-off Disney World.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Sunday, Cruddy Sunday
programme.desc: When Homer organizes a trip to the Superbowl, the tickets turn out to be bogus.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Simpsons Spin-Off Showcase
programme.desc: Troy McClure introduces three possible spin-offs from the series, including 'Chief Wiggum P.I.,' 'The Love-Matic Grampa' and 'The Simpson Family Smile-Time Variety Hour.'

programme.title: The Simpsons: Lisa Gets the Blues
programme.desc: When the Simpsons' flight to Gainesville gets re-routed to New Orleans, Lisa is forced to face her failures, and regain her confidence as a jazz musician. Meanwhile, Bart is intrigued by the voodoo in Louisiana.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Old Man and the Key
programme.desc: Grampa Simpson tries to win the affection of a sultry new woman at his retirement home.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Oh Brother, Where Art Thou?
programme.desc: Homer finds out that he has a half-brother in Detroit who is a successful carmaker.

programme.title: The Simpsons: He Loves to Fly and He D'ohs
programme.desc: After Homer saves Mr. Burns from drowning, Burns thanks him by taking him on a whirlwind trip to Chicago on his private plane. After returning home, Homer makes it his mission to find a job that offers him the chance to fly on a corporate jet.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Mad About the Toy
programme.desc: When Marge and Homer leave Grampa to babysit the kids, some little green army men trigger a PTSD episode, sending the family on a journey to Grampa’s past to when he was a post-war toy model.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Double Double Boy in Trouble
programme.desc: Bart meets his exact look-alike, Simon Woosterfield, who happens to be a member of the richest family in Springfield. When the boys decide to switch places, Bart learns that his wealthy 'siblings' are plotting his demise so they can get his inheritance.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Homerland
programme.desc: In a nod to the hit cable series 'Homeland,' Lisa suspects Homer may have been 'turned' when he returns a changed man from a nuclear power plant operating convention. So she enlists the services of the FBI.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Any Given Sundance
programme.desc: When Lisa's documentary on her family premieres at the Sundance Film Festival, Homer, Marge and Bart are embarrassed by the candid behind-the-scenes look at their lives.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Fland Canyon
programme.desc: Homer recalls a tense family vacation to the Grand Canyon with the Flanders family. At first, the Flanders' perfection puts the families at odds, but when they get stranded and Ned and Bart go for help, the families finally bond.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Viva Ned Flanders
programme.desc: Feeling as though life is passing him by, Ned takes a gambling vacation in Sin City.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Co-Dependent's Day
programme.desc: Homer and Marge go on a drinking bender after they take a wine-tasting trip.

programme.title: The Simpsons: I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can
programme.desc: Lisa wins the spelling bee and a scout from the Spellympics recruits her.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Beyond Blunderdome
programme.desc: Homer gives his honest opinion to Mel Gibson at a test screening of Mel's new film.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Angry Dad: The Movie
programme.desc: Bart's cartoon about an angry dad is turned into a movie and quickly becomes a critics' favourite. When Russell Brand presents the Golden Globe to Bart's film, Homer usurps the podium and gives his own acceptance speech.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Little Big Mom
programme.desc: Homer impulsively takes the family skiing but Marge lands herself in the hospital.

programme.title: Big Sharks Rule

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Florida Doomsday Castle
programme.desc: A strange bunker near the Kennedy Space Center once served a top-secret military function should doomsday ever come to the Sunshine State. Experts use declassified intel to reveal the sinister purpose of this forbidding facility and why it was abandoned.

programme.title: Hubble: Thirty Years of Discovery
programme.desc: For thirty years, NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has discovered new alien worlds, black holes, and the age of the universe itself. NASA astronauts reveal the secret history of the life-or-death missions to keep this complex machine working.

programme.title: NASA and SpaceX: Journey to the Future

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Hunt for Alien Evidence
programme.desc: The discovery of extraterrestrial life might face an impossible challenge: the physics of the universe itself. Using cutting-edge technology, experts might be on the verge of a groundbreaking find and the evidence could already be in hand.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Inside El Chapo's Escape
programme.desc: Satellite images reveal how El Chapo escaped from a high security prison in Mexico. Mysterious patterns in the Australian outback and the Kalahari Desert spark a major scientific investigation. A giant black stain appears in a war-torn area of Iraq.

programme.title: What on Earth?: World's Deadliest Threats
programme.desc: The world's most dangerous threats are in top-secret locations all over the planet, and using cutting-edge technology, experts reveal the truth behind these sinister mysteries from space.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Cannibal Death Pit
programme.desc: A strange circular forest is found next to a notorious cannibal site. Then, when an eerie crater is spotted in Siberia, experts uncover if it could be evidence of Nikola Tesla's doomsday device.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Lost Tombs of the Gold Kingdom
programme.desc: Strange satellite images taken above the Nile Valley could point investigators to long-lost relics buried in an unexplored city of the dead. What they find could reveal new secrets of Kush, a once-mighty kingdom of untold gold and treasure.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ghost Town Terror
programme.desc: When satellites discover a strange abandoned city frozen in time, experts investigate what caused the people who lived here to disappear overnight. Using cutting-edge tech, they follow fresh leads to uncover if this mysterious city has a dark secret.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ruins of Cannibal City
programme.desc: When an extreme drought hits Spain, the ruins of a long-lost civilization suddenly emerge, and using cutting-edge technology, experts investigate evidence of ancient cannibalism.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Hunt for the Ark of the Covenant
programme.desc: New clues taken from long-lost maps offer fresh leads in the hunt for the Ark of the Covenant, and using the latest hi-tech archaeological methods, experts retrace its steps through the Holy Land, Egypt, and Ethiopia to make a game-changing discovery.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Killing Meriwether Lewis
programme.desc: Meriwether Lewis was the man who teamed with William Clark to map the lands west of the Mississippi, but he died in mysterious circumstances. Experts reveal a vast conspiracy that could prove that one of America's greatest explorers was murdered.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Ghost Ship of the Damned
programme.desc: A long-lost treasure map claims to know the whereabouts of a missing ship buried with gold and silver in the California desert, and using cutting-edge technology, a team of experts use this new evidence to get close to a game-changing discovery.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Egypt's Land of the Gods
programme.desc: New evidence from one of the world's oldest maps offers clues to a long-lost civilization of untold treasure, and this cutting-edge expedition might finally prove that this mysterious kingdom is the ancestral home of the ancient Egyptians.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Mystery of the American Maya
programme.desc: New evidence suggests the Maya civilization did not suddenly vanish, but that they instead still survived, they just resettled in the United States. Using cutting-edge tech, experts reveal what really happened to these long-lost people.

programme.title: See No Evil: Captured on Camera

programme.title: See No Evil: Watching Amy Lord
programme.desc: 24-year-old former cheerleader Amy Lord is missing in South Boston. Police first find her burned car and then... her body. Incredible footage from five ATMs and outside her apartment building captures the exact moment she was taken by her killer.

programme.title: See No Evil: Beth's Last Party
programme.desc: On December 23rd 2009, Beth von Steinner goes missing. Days later, her frozen body is found in a trash pile behind her house. With no witness or suspects, only CCTV footage can unlock the mystery of who killed her.

programme.title: See No Evil: Fire Angel
programme.desc: When she interrupts a burglary at home, Diane Pranske is abducted and left for dead across state lines. Detectives turn to surveillance video to find her assailant.

programme.desc: The morning of his anniversary, Mike Williams goes duck hunting. When he fails to return home that afternoon, an all-out search commences. But it's only the beginning of a sordid mystery that will take close to 20 years to solve.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Gone in 90 Seconds
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old Arizona girl vanishes while waiting for an ice cream truck, it's clear she's been abducted. The search for her leads to a trail of suspects, making it the largest missing child investigation in Arizona history.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: The Cheerleader and the Hitman
programme.desc: When a widower and father of three is found dead outside his Long Island home, police have reason to believe shady business dealings may have caused his murder. But then a tip leads to three unlikely suspects, exposing dark family secrets.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Britain at Bay
programme.desc: By the end of June 1940, Hitler was victorious in Western Europe. Nazi Germany now controlled Denmark, Norway, the Netherlands, Belgium and France. Only Britain remained defiant. But the Fuehrer was certain that it could not hold out for long.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Lightning War
programme.desc: Hitler's armed forces took just four weeks to conquer Poland. Their opponent was not only outnumbered both in men and weapons, but facing a new type of warfare - Lightning War or Blitzkrieg - for which it was totally unprepared.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Hitler Strikes East
programme.desc: By the autumn of 1940, Hitler's forces were supreme in Western Europe. Britain remained undefeated but the country was isolated and Hitler no longer considered it a threat. The Fuhrer was ready for his greatest gamble - Operation Barbarossa.

programme.title: Against the Odds: A Chance in Hell: The Battle of Ramadi
programme.desc: When Colonel McFarland arrived in Iraq, Al-Qaeda had already declared Ramadi the capital of a new caliphate. The soldiers of McFarland's Ready First Combat Team fight to give American a victory.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Death Ridges of Peleliu
programme.desc: The First Marine Division forged a ferocious struggle with the Japanese at Peleliu. It would take 74 days and cost 10,000 American lives but the blood and courage of this small band of heroes would ultimately break the back of the enemy.

programme.title: Against the Odds: Fighting Fox: Road to Hell
programme.desc: The paratroopers of Fighting Fox Company, 2nd Battalion, 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment fought and died from Normandy to Hitler's Eagle's Nest. Their sheer determination against the enemy was matched only by their devotion to one another.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Untold Story of the Rescue at Dogshead
programme.desc: Charlie Company of the 1st Cavalry Division wanders into an NVA stronghold. A blistering attack leaves supplies low, casualties mounting and the enemy circle tightening. Team Alpha, hearing the distress calls, volunteers to try the difficult rescue.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Death Ridges of Peleliu

programme.title: Air Aces: George Beurling
programme.desc: Canadian George Beurling broke every rule in the book yet became one of the greatest fighter pilots of World War II.

programme.title: Air Aces: Douglas Bader
programme.desc: In July 1940, Britain's most celebrated air ace, Douglas Bader, and his ragtag squadron of Canadian fighter pilots, went into battle against Hitler's mighty Luftwaffe.

programme.title: Air Aces: Wing Walker
programme.desc: The crew of Lancaster ME669 had one of the most dangerous jobs in the Air Force.

programme.title: America: Facts vs. Fiction: Patriots and Presidents
programme.desc: Learn the truth behind some of America's most powerful men and the birth of the country they governed.

programme.title: America: Facts vs. Fiction: Bonus Round: Vices and Vacations
programme.desc: On this special edition of America: Facts vs. Fiction, we explore the myths surrounding our favorite vices and learn the facts behind some of our favorite holidays.

programme.title: WWII in the Pacific: Island Warfare
programme.desc: By the summer of 1942, the US has the upper hand in the war with Japan. It has achieved naval superiority. In the spring of 1945 the loss of life is great for both sides but Japan refuses to surrender. US Chiefs desperately look for a way to end the way.

programme.title: WWII in the Pacific: No Surrender
programme.desc: The war is turning America's way. Yet Japan is determined to kill as many Americans as possible and fight to the death. The US must determine if it has the stomach for the fight, or if there is a secret weapon that offers a quick way out.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Rockin' it in Rocket City
programme.desc: Lucas goes to Huntsville, Alabama to build the lifelong dream pool of a retiring military couple. Lucas designs the largest grotto enclosed spa and widest waterfall of his career. This build also requires a tornado shelter.

programme.title: Insane Pools: Off the Deep End: Green With Envy
programme.desc: Lucas and the crew have their work cut out for them as a client's demands leave him reluctant. A Caribbean destination that includes green rocks and a sand bottom salt-water pool is the client's request.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Golden Years Family Camp
programme.desc: After buying her parents' old summer camp in Bridgton, a mother calls on the crew to help her completely renovate the home for her parents to retire in full-time. With a $40,000 budget and an open time line, the crew transform it into a retirement space.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Changing of the Guard's Camp
programme.desc: Recently Jeff and his wife Leah inherited the cabin he grew up in from his mother but it has been dormant for nearly 10 years and needs some major repairs and upgrades.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Desert Pond Hideaway
programme.desc: Chase and the team travel to a secluded piece of land close to Desert Pond to help a couple turn their forgotten shack into a relaxing retreat. The couple have big ambitions leaving the crew stretching a $30,000 budget in only four weeks.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Inspirations Brewing
programme.desc: When Chase and his brother-in-law learn that the owner of their latest cabin project works at Allagash Brewing Company, they're excited to check out the brewery for inspiration as they turn the weekend getaway into a three-season home away from home.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Holmes Makes It Right: Retooled: To Mike, From Aggie
programme.desc: A nurse who works countless hours at a brain injury center is too busy supporting her family to fix the many problems in her new house. Her mother-in-law Aggie is so desperate to help she delivers a letter straight to Mike's mailbox.

programme.title: Holmes Makes It Right: Retooled: Broken Record
programme.desc: Mike and his team come to the rescue of Sheila and Val, a couple scammed out of their money, their kitchen and their trust by a notorious contracting criminal who would occasionally do one good job to lure other unsuspecting clients.

programme.title: Holmes Makes It Right: Retooled: Investment Gate
programme.desc: Mike Holmes helps a couple pick up the pieces after getting conned out of $300,000 by a contractor friend who has conned millions from other families.

programme.title: Holmes Makes It Right: Below Board
programme.desc: Mike Holmes discovers that a house that looks good on the outside is hiding a rotting secret just below the surface, and now it’s a problem that threatens an entire neighbourhood.

programme.title: Holmes Makes It Right: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a contractor holds Laura and Greg's new kitchen hostage for four months, even threatening to put a lien on their home, the desperate couple turn to the one contractor they can trust.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: From Texas to Tennessee: A Look Back
programme.desc: Mark Bowe looks back at an amazing season of Barnwood Builders. From Texas to Tennessee and from Ohio to the crew's home in West Virginia, this was a season full of big surprises with new faces, new places and a brand new Boneyard.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: The Johnny Jett Special
programme.desc: Machinist, artist and craftsman Johnny Jett is honoured by The Barnwood Builders in this one-hour special. Johnny's most unforgettable moments are highlighted as well as previously unaired footage featuring West Virginia's favorite Renaissance man.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Grandma's Cabin
programme.desc: Mark and the guys save the logs from a fire-damaged home in West Virginia. Mark promises the family who sold him the cabin that he will honor their history here. Once the logs are salvaged, the cabin is rebuilt on the Boneyard for a client in Montana.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Perfect Cabin, Perfectly Restored
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders discover one of the finest cabins they've ever seen. They take it down in West Virginia, then restore it in South Carolina. Mark returns to see the amazing home fully finished.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Desert Mountain House
programme.desc: A creative family builds a custom home where the mountains and desert collide in Colorado's last frontier. The dream project quickly turns into a job when the harsh terrain combines with the demanding pace they've set for themselves.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Meat
programme.desc: Roger Mooking visits the most creative chefs in the U.S. to get their mouthwatering secrets for meaty celebrations that include whole hog barbecue, buffalo meatloaf and more.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Manhattan
programme.desc: Casey Webb's tasty trek brings him to Manhattan for historic pizza-by-the-slice, a transcendent take on an Old World baked good and over five pounds of undefeated dessert in the Giant Ice Cream Scoop Challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Denver
programme.desc: Adam visits the Mile-High City for burgers with enough toppings to fill a dartboard, some Rocky Mountain oysters, and a showdown with the biggest burrito in the West.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Francisco
programme.desc: Adam devours a Mission Style Super Burrito, ginormous sandwiches, and a two-gallon monster sundae.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation: Burlington, VT
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to the small town of Burlington, Vermont, for some mammoth eats, including a beer-infused bagel feast and a 3.5-pound breakfast sandwich challenge.

programme.title: Good Eats: American Classics X: Chicken Parm
programme.desc: Alton Brown makes the argument that Italian food was actually invented in America, and that chicken parmesan is the dish where it all began. Yet, an Italian canned tomato makes it all possible.

programme.title: Good Eats: Pretzel Logic
programme.desc: Alton Brown recreates the traditional soft pretzel in all its crisp, chewy, golden brown glory.

programme.title: Good Eats: Reloaded: Oat Cuisine: The Reload
programme.desc: Alton Brown 'reloads' this episode from 2001 with new recipes for his favourite grain, including a no-cook overnight oat pudding and a flavor-charged granola, now with more nutrition!

programme.title: Good Eats: Reloaded: A Cake on Every Plate: The Reload
programme.desc: Alton Brown 'reloads' this episode from 2003 and reinvents the cake that so many folks complained about way back when. This time, the cake isn't just good: it's golden.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: A self-made millionaire pursues a romance with Monica; Chandler tries to quit smoking.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Ross faces another heartache when Rachel goes out on her first date since their break-up.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Dollhouse
programme.desc: Monica inherits a dollhouse, Joey likes an actress, and Rachel's boss dates Chandler.

programme.title: Friends: The One With a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: It's a dream come true for Monica when Pete buys a restaurant and makes her head chef.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Screamer
programme.desc: Tommy, a seemingly nice young man, accompanies Rachel to the opening of Joey's play.

programme.title: Friends: The One With Ross' Thing
programme.desc: Ross develops a mysterious skin abnormality that his doctor cannot identify. Phoebe can't decide whether to date a fireman or a teacher. Monica believes that Pete is going to propose.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Ultimate Fighting Championship
programme.desc: Pete's decision to compete in ultimate fighting forces Monica to make an important decision about their relationship. Phoebe sets Ross up on a date with a woman who used to be bald.

programme.title: Friends: The One at the Beach
programme.desc: Phoebe invites the gang to a Long Island beach house while she searches for her father. While there, Ross learns that Rachel still has feelings for him.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Jellyfish
programme.desc: After Ross reunites with Rachel, she wants him to take the blame for their breakup.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Cat
programme.desc: Phoebe is convinced that a stray cat is really her deceased adoptive mom reincarnated.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Cuffs
programme.desc: Chandler has a sexy encounter with Rachel's boss which leaves him in a predicament. Rachel is torn knowing she will put her job at risks if she helps him out.

programme.title: American Dad!: White Rice
programme.desc: After an argument, Stan suggests he and Francine see a counsellor, who hypnotizes Francine and reawakens her lifelong dream of being a comedian. Steve brings home a potential love interest for Klaus.

programme.title: American Dad!: There Will Be Bad Blood
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving Day, Stan invites his half-brother, Rusty, over to show off his cornucopia of wealth and good fortune. When Stan learns that Rusty is far wealthier than he initially led on, Stan tries to take what he feels is his.

programme.title: Native America: Nature to Nations
programme.desc: Explore the rise of great American nations, from monarchies to democracies. Investigate lost cities in Mexico, a temple in Peru, a potlatch ceremony in the Pacific Northwest and a tapestry of shell beads in upstate New York.

programme.title: BBC World News: America
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Katty Kay anchors BBC AMERICA's award-winning hour-long, U.S.-targeted nightly newscast.

programme.title: BBC World News Outside Source
programme.desc: Live from the heart of the BBC newsroom, the programme that gathers the latest information as it arrives from news wires, video feeds and social media on the biggest stories of the day.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: An analysis of current events with a news summary, discussions, and news features.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots: Beyond the Pale
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. explores the Jewish heritages of actor Jeff Goldblum, radio host Terry Gross and comedian Marc Maron, uncovering previously unknown stories that show each shares much more with their ancestors than a religious tradition.

programme.title: Viral: Antisemitism in Four Mutations
programme.desc: Antisemitism in the U.S. and Europe is rising and worsening in ways not seen since the 1930s. It comes in the forms of vandalism, social media abuse, assault and murder. Like a virus, it mutates and evolves across cultures.

programme.title: Frontline: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: A landmark documentary about some of the last survivors of the Holocaust. Only children at the time, these now elderly survivors reflect on how the trauma of the Holocaust has affected the rest of their lives.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: In-depth conversations with global thought leaders and cultural influencers.

programme.title: Uncertainty: Why We're So Anxious About Anxiety
programme.desc: Explore anxiety disorders, including their prevalence, signs and symptoms, and why the conversation about mental health is so important.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots: Beyond the Pale
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. explores the Jewish heritages of actor Jeff Goldblum, radio host Terry Gross and comedian Marc Maron, uncovering previously unknown stories that show each shares much more with their ancestors than a religious tradition.

programme.title: Frontline: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: A landmark documentary about some of the last survivors of the Holocaust. Only children at the time, these now elderly survivors reflect on how the trauma of the Holocaust has affected the rest of their lives.

programme.title: Independent Lens: Eating Up Easter
programme.desc: See how climate change and a booming tourism trade threaten the fragile economy of Rapa Nui, also known as Easter Island, and meet the local artists, ecologists and developers balancing their strong cultural heritage with modern-day challenges.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow: Charleston (Hour Two)
programme.desc: An 1899 Oscar Wilde manuscript poem and a 1960 Rene Portocarrero 'Catedral' oil are showcased.

programme.title: Sit and Be Fit: Pamper Your Feet
programme.desc: These exercises stimulate your feet and keep them healthy.

programme.title: Peg + Cat: The Potty Problem/ The Butter Problem
programme.desc: Peg and Cat learn certain math skills by doing farm chores to compete in a butter sculpting contest.

programme.title: Cyberchase: Shari Spotter and the Cosmic Crumpets
programme.desc: Shari Spotter makes the Cosmic Crumpets before Hacker becomes the most powerful magician.

programme.title: Molly of Denali: Bird in the Hand/ Bye-Bye Birdie
programme.desc: Molly and Tooey think they’ve discovered a ghost after a strange noise follows them from Spooky Hose all the way to the Trading Post’s Bunkhouse. Molly and Trini tag along with Nina on trip to Kenai National Park to see real, live puffins!

programme.title: Nova: Deadliest Tornadoes
programme.desc: Explore the science behind the April 2011 tornadoes that left a trail of destruction across the United States.

programme.title: History Detectives: 1775 Almanac, Exercise Records, Moon Museum
programme.desc: A 1775 Almanac indicates that the revolution may have strained family ties. A 1920s fitness guru stars in a production called 'Get Thin to Music.' Investigating if Andy Warhol's art was ever smuggled to the moon.

programme.title: Rivers of Life: The Nile
programme.desc: The Nile is the world's longest river. From elephants and leopards to extreme kayakers and ancient wonders, its great length provides a lifeline for Africa's wildest beasts and for some of the world's most incredible cultures.

programme.title: Food: Delicious Science: Food on the Brain
programme.desc: Discover how the chemistry in our food impacts our brains and creates our deepest cravings.

programme.title: Nova: Deadliest Tornadoes
programme.desc: Explore the science behind the April 2011 tornadoes that left a trail of destruction across the United States.

programme.title: American Experience: Mr. Tornado
programme.desc: Meet pioneering meteorologist Ted Fujita, who transformed our understanding of tornados. Learn how we can protect ourselves and our communities.

programme.title: Masterpiece Classic: Little Women
programme.desc: Set against the backdrop of the Civil War, this story follows sisters Jo, Meg, Beth and Amy March on their journey from childhood to adulthood. With the help of their mother, the girls navigate what it means to be a young woman.

programme.title: American Masters: Louisa May Alcott: The Woman Behind 'Little Women'
programme.desc: 'Little Women' author Louisa May Alcott is immediately recognized as a morally upstanding citizen, but what many do not know is that she was a democratic free thinker and had progressive ideas about women's rights.

programme.title: Orchard House: Home of Little Women
programme.desc: The 350-year-old home in Concord, Massachusetts where Little Women was written is highlighted. Orchard House uniquely inspired Louisa May Alcott to write the beloved novel at a desk in her room that her father made especially for her.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Ritual
programme.desc: The death of a Nigerian boy leads detectives in a search for the Santeria practitioner.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Intimidation Game
programme.desc: When a female employee is assaulted at a gamer convention, Detective Tutuola investigates the crime. A female game developer refuses to delay her game launch, and the cops must 'level up' to protect her.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Granting Immunity
programme.desc: Pornographic photos from a teen girl's house party quickly go viral. Barbara's efforts to arrest the students responsible for posting the images are halted during a measles' outbreak at the school.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Perverted Justice
programme.desc: Bayard Ellis takes on the case of a man incarcerated for incest and rape whose daughter, the victim and star witness when she was a child, wants to recant her testimony and set her father free.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Townhouse Incident
programme.desc: Benson is taken hostage when she tries to stop a violent home invasion.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Nationwide Manhunt
programme.desc: Chicago P.D. detectives Lindsay and Dawson join the SVU in the hunt for two escaped murderers they helped to convict.

programme.title: The Broderick Files: Betty Broderick
programme.desc: A look back at one of the most riveting cases of the 1990s: the Betty Broderick murders.

programme.title: Modern Family: Blasts From the Past
programme.desc: Phil and Claire decide to take a cycling tour through Italy before they become grandparents, and Dylan introduces them to his mom who has an interesting past.

programme.title: Modern Family: Whanex?
programme.desc: While preparing a presentation for his students about their future goals, Cam must also contend with a bully at the school and Jay is having a little technical trouble during his video conference.

programme.title: Modern Family: We Need to Talk About Lily
programme.desc: Phil may have conquered his biggest real estate challenge when he thinks he found Pepper his dream home. Meanwhile, in an attempt to connect with Lily, Mitch and Cam get invited to an exclusive event for her favourite vlogger.

programme.title: Modern Family: Red Alert
programme.desc: Lily has reached a womanly milestone that Cam and Mitch are not prepared for at all so they call in reinforcements.

programme.title: Modern Family: The Wild
programme.desc: Mitch, Cam and Phil crash Jay's annual hiking trip to find an elusive bald eagle after Gloria asks them to keep an eye on him. Meanwhile, Gloria, Alex, Claire and Haley get together to plan her baby shower.

programme.title: Modern Family: Stand by Your Man
programme.desc: Dylan and Luke are really impressed with Bill's tales of heroism and survival as a firefighter, which makes Phil feel emasculated. To impress the guys, he invites them over for a little male-bonding that goes terribly wrong.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Favor, Affection, Malice or Ill-Will
programme.desc: When Lindsay is tipped off about a grieving father looking to avenge his daughter's death, Olinsky goes undercover as a hitman-for-hire. Meanwhile, Ruzek comes back after finishing his undercover assignment, only to have Voight knock him down to patrol.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Emotional Proximity
programme.desc: Olinsky's daughter remains in critical condition following a massive fire, leaving Intelligence on edge, but more determined than ever to track down the suspect.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Remember the Devil
programme.desc: After being tipped off about a woman running barefoot through the woods, Intelligence is led to a kidnapping victim locked up in a shipping container.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A Little Bit of Light
programme.desc: When Chicago's 'Nightcrawler' Bobby Trent is murdered over a single piece of footage, Intelligence must investigate one of Chicago's wealthiest families, uncovering a lineage of old money and secrets.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation: The Case of the Cross-Dressing Carp
programme.desc: Grissom and his team discover a young man, dressed like a woman, strangled to death by hanging.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles: Field of Fire
programme.desc: The team searches for a former marine and expert sniper who escaped a veteran's hospital when they discover his connection to the leader of an extremist group.

programme.title: NCIS: A Desperate Man
programme.desc: When a navy lieutenant commander is found dead in a building under construction, NCIS must search for a killer while dealing with interruptions from her distraught detective husband. Meanwhile, Ray's return has Ziva thinking about her future.

programme.title: NCIS: Kill Ari, Part 1
programme.desc: While the NCIS team deals with Kate's death, Gibbs's determination to finally put an end to Ari's reign of terror mounts. Though he has no concrete evidence that the sniper was Ari, Gibbs is haunted by the thought that the bullet was meant for him.

programme.title: NCIS: Kill Ari, Part II
programme.desc: Gibbs, who is still haunted by images of Kate's death, is determined to stop Ari after he and Director Shepard are the target of sniper fire and Ducky is lured out of the NCIS headquarters by the terrorist.

programme.title: NCIS: Shalom
programme.desc: As each team member deals with the absence of Gibbs in their own way, Ziva's past comes back to haunt her when she witnesses an assassination of a military attaché by her former Mossad partner. Ziva then seeks refuge at the Israeli Embassy.

programme.title: NCIS: Truth or Consequences
programme.desc: As the hunt for Ziva's replacement begins, Gibbs and his remaining team members must head back to work with the past still weighing heavily on their minds.

programme.title: NCIS: Reunion
programme.desc: The team discovers the bizarre death of a marine and his two friends may be linked to their past but its Gibbs who ends up taking a trip down memory lane.

programme.title: NCIS: Good Cop, Bad Cop
programme.desc: When a marine's body surfaces halfway around the world, the investigation forces Ziva to dig up a buried chapter of her life.

programme.title: NCIS: Code of Conduct
programme.desc: When a marine known for his practical jokes is found dead on Halloween Eve, the NCIS team discovers that the victim's good-natured pranks may have inspired ghoulish retribution. Meanwhile, Ziva tries to adjust to her new role on the team.

programme.title: NCIS: Safe Harbor
programme.desc: When a coast guard officer is gunned down on a cargo vessel, the team searches for the killer, but they find a Lebanese family seeking refuge on American soil instead. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs.

programme.title: Movie: Despicable Me
programme.desc: A criminal mastermind uses three orphans in his grand scheme to pull off the biggest heist in the world, but their love profoundly changes him for the better.

programme.title: Movie: Despicable Me 2

programme.title: Movie: Shrek

programme.desc: Sam and Dean listen to a message on John's cell phone from a woman named Ellen and decide to track her down. At a small town carnival, a clown is persuading children to let him into their home so he can murder their parents.

programme.desc: Nick Cannon and Baby Ariel battle it out in "Eat that Ass Up," "Talking Spit," "Rest in Peace," and the Wildstyle battle. BJ the Chicago Kid performs.

programme.title: Martin: Lockin' Boots
